snippet.title: mPower Moments: Hard Work and Limitless Opportunities with Fannie Mae’s Priscilla Almodovar
snippet.description:
“Hard work is where my confidence comes from.”
 
mPower Founder Marcia M. Davies sits down with Priscilla Almodovar, CEO at Fannie Mae, for an insightful interview on Priscilla’s career, including her “dream job” at Fannie Mae. She also shares the many lessons she has learned and emphasizes how hard work and effort have helped her thrive. Furthermore, Priscilla also talks about the importance of affordable housing, and how it is her mission to ensure that her employees feel motivated and connected to Fannie Mae's mission of creating affordable housing opportunities.

Furthermore, Priscilla also talks about the importance of affordable housing, and how it is her mission to ensure that her employees feel motivated and connected to Fannie Mae's mission of creating affordable housing opportunities.

snippet.title: Jacob Elordi & Colman Domingo | Actors on Actors
snippet.description:

snippet.title: Tarik Saleh - Best screenplay for "Boy from heaven " Swedish Film Awards
snippet.description:
We met Tarik Saleh at the Swedish Film Awards, where he won the prize for best screenplay for "Boy from heaven". But what happened after he released the movie, what do Egypt think about his? How has Tarik life changed after this?

snippet.title: Subject -  Official Trailer
snippet.description:
Streaming now on @DocPlay: https://www.docplay.com/shows/subject

Subject explores the life-altering experience of sharing one’s life on screen through key participants of acclaimed documentaries The Staircase, Hoop Dreams, The Wolfpack, Capturing the Friedmans, and The Square. These erstwhile documentary “stars” reveal the highs and lows of their experiences as well as the everyday realities of having their lives put under a microscope. Also featuring commentary from such influential names in the doc world as Kirsten Johnson, Sam Pollard, Thom Powers and Sonya Childress, the film unpacks vital issues around the ethics and responsibility inherent in documentary filmmaking. As tens of millions of people consume documentaries in an unprecedented "golden era," Subject urges audiences to consider the often profound impact on their participants. #Subject 


More from Madman Films: http://www.madmanfilms.com.au

snippet.title: Bill Ackman: Biggest Risky Business Decision I Ever Made | Cash is Trash
snippet.description:
Bill Ackman: Biggest Risky Business Decision I Ever Made

Discover the factors that led to the financial crisis in this eye-opening video. Explore how management teams ventured into risky businesses and made questionable decisions, including betting on securities prices and issuing riskier loans. Learn about the influence of Congress in pressing for riskier mortgages, and how Fannie Mae sought permission to engage in riskier endeavors. Uncover the role of Barney Franks and the resulting insolvency of these entities when home prices started to decline. Gain insights into the consequences of short selling and the lingering effects of the crisis.

snippet.title: 5 FILMES QUE ACHEI QUE IA AMAR, MAS ODIEI
snippet.description:

La siguiente peli de la que quiero hablar es The Virgin Suicides, pero ese video será un poco más ordenado i promise
La neta ya estoy arrepintiéndome de hacerlo tan improvisado porque se me están ocurriendo más cosas quE no dije en el video JAJA😭

snippet.title: Could Fannie Mae & Freddie Mac Do a Reverse Waterfall if US Government Decides to Sell?
snippet.description:
The debt ceiling may make the US government sell it's stakes in Fannie Mae and Freddie Mac. 

In this video, I lay out the theoretical upside for these stocks if it results in these stocks trading for the value in their business. 

Remember: This video is not investment advice and the stocks discussed and scenarios laid out have risk.

Want to take a deeper dive? 
https://paulmampilly.substack.com/

Looking for guidance on our stocks👇
https://www.atgdigital.media/
